# A repeat `lock acquire` by the SAME holder on a path/mode it already holds is
# idempotent (regression: used to error with a plain conflict indistinguishable
# from "someone else has it" -- e.g. a session-resumed agent that lost track of
# its own held lock). A different holder still genuinely conflicts, and that
# conflict error now names who holds it.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze lock acquire src/main.go --as alice --ttl 30m --json
stdout '"id":\s*"l1"'

# Re-acquire by the SAME holder: succeeds, same lock ID, no duplicate.
exec breeze lock acquire src/main.go --as alice --ttl 30m --json
stdout '"id":\s*"l1"'
exec breeze lock list --json
stdout '"id":\s*"l1"'
! stdout '"id":\s*"l2"'

# A DIFFERENT holder still conflicts -- and the error now names alice.
! exec breeze lock acquire src/main.go --as bob --ttl 30m
stderr 'already held by "alice"'

exec breeze stop
